title: test: simplify and fix "podman volume: exec/noexec"
body:
test: simplify and fix "podman volume: exec/noexec"
    
A newer runc (v1.1.x) changed the error message again, so simplify the
check to look for a common denominator of all these errors.
    
Note that due to an entirely different issue [1] the test still fails when
using runc, but this is being fixed elsewhere ([2], [3]). This will
presumably be fixed in runc 1.1.4 and runc 1.2.0.
    
[1] https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/3520
[2] https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/pull/3522
[3] https://go-review.googlesource.com/c/go/+/414824

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
NONE
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe*, *kolyshkin* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@kolyshkin Why is this a draft?
LGTM

/hold

I need to look into all the RHELs where this will land: `runc` may not update at the same rate, and this would break RHEL gating tests. I will do so Tuesday.
Ah, okay, I see why this is passing: we're not actually testing `runc` in CI. @cevich do you know the context behind this? I thought old-fedora was supposed to be configured with `runc`?
Ubuntu was testing with runc and fedora runc, was my understanding.
I can't remember if it was fedora or ubuntu that was supposed to use runc. Whatever we may have intended, we have zero testing of runc in CI right now. I don't know if that's intentional or not.

NONONONONO! Sorry, I meant to update this yesterday. This can't merge, not until we start testing with runc again. And once we do, we need to figure out a way to make it actually work, because as this PR stands, it will not actually work with runc.

@kolyshkin I've incorporated a modified version of your changes into [my chaotic evil runc PR](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14972/files#diff-8e27a9e0ab1eb9cc49e6d4ee7b86689bb5b76e4fc7d80187185b4470351b7845). Note that I removed the `OCI` part, because the `runc` in use on Ubuntu at this moment does not include `OCI` in its output. As submitted, I think the test should work with `crun`, `old-runc`, and `new-runc` but would welcome your feedback.

I did not incorporate your comment fixes, but I like them, so please feel free to re-push this as a `CI:DOCS` PR. Thanks for your patience and understanding, and **especially** thank you for submitting this PR! Your exit-code change raised all the alarm bells in my head that led to my discovering our hole in testing runc. Without this PR, we might've gone months without discovering that, and #14972 would've been worse than it already is.

@kolyshkin: PR needs rebase.

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

> Note that I removed the `OCI` part, because the `runc` in use on Ubuntu at this moment does not include `OCI` in its output. As submitted, I think the test should work with `crun`, `old-runc`, and `new-runc` but would welcome your feedback.

The `OCI` part is added by podman, not runc.

This won't work without a runc fix from https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/pull/3522 (as noted in this PR description)
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
I think this was handled in #14972, apart from the language fix (which is still worth doing)
